title: Intense Malaysian Oats Bowl
description: A buttery, snappy, cozy Malaysian dish featuring porridge oats infused with shio-koji cure, served in a cazuela for a whole30-friendly breakfast or snack.
instructions: In a clay pot, combine 1 cup rolled oats, 2 cups vegetable broth, 1/4 cup coconut milk, and 1 tablespoon shio-koji; stir until well mixed.
Let the mixture rest for 5 minutes to allow the oats to absorb the shio-koji flavor.
Place the pot over medium heat and bring to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally.
After 10 minutes, add 1 tablespoon butter, 1/4 teaspoon sea salt, and 1/8 teaspoon cracked black pepper; stir until the butter melts and the oats are creamy.
Crack a large egg into a small bowl, whisk lightly, then pour slowly into the pot while stirring continuously to create silky ribbons; cook for 2 minutes until the egg is set.
Remove the pot from heat and let it cool for a minute.
Transfer the oats bowl to a serving dish, drizzle with 1 tablespoon lime juice, and top with 2 tablespoons roasted peanuts and chopped scallions.
Serve immediately, optionally with sliced cucumber and a wedge of lime on the side.
